response.extracted_text: The Economic & Structural Reality U.S. national health expenditures now exceed $4.5 trillion annually, yet the way physicians deliver care has shifted just as dramatically as the dollars involved. The dominant structural force: a sweeping migration from independent private practice into large, integrated health systems. Independent Practice Once the norm — physicians as autonomous small-business owners Consolidation Era Rapid acquisition by health systems, insurers, and private equity Employed Majority Over 70% of physicians are now employed rather than independent
response.layout_description: The slide is split with a white text-heavy left panel and a full-height aerial hospital/city photo on the right. The left side contains a large title at top, a paragraph below it, and a pale green three-section callout box near the bottom.
text_blocks: U.S. national health expenditures now exceed $4.5 trillion annually, yet the way physicians deliver care has shifted just as dramatically as the dollars involved. The dominant structural force: a sweeping migration from independent private practice into large, integrated health systems.
Independent Practice Once the norm — physicians as autonomous small-business owners
Consolidation Era Rapid acquisition by health systems, insurers, and private equity
Employed Majority Over 70% of physicians are now employed rather than independent
